timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Oil lamps spread in Mesopotamia
description: Early wick lamps burning animal fat or plant oils became a durable technology for extending activity after sunset in Mesopotamia.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Candles appear in ancient Egypt
description: Early candles made from rushes soaked in fat gave ancient Egyptians a portable source of light.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Greek and Roman lamp forms mature
description: Terracotta and metal oil lamps with enclosed reservoirs and better wicks became common around the Mediterranean world.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Han-era lanterns become established in China
description: Paper and silk lantern traditions developed in imperial China, making carried light more practical and culturally significant.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Beeswax candles gain prestige in Rome
description: Beeswax candles, cleaner and less smoky than tallow, became valued in religious and elite settings in the Roman world.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Improved glass lamps spread in the Islamic world
description: Glass lampmaking and refined oil-burning techniques helped cities and mosques sustain brighter interior illumination.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Rushlights become common in medieval Europe
description: Cheap rushlights provided basic household illumination for ordinary people across much of Europe.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Argand lamp is introduced
description: Aimé Argand's circular wick lamp produced a brighter and steadier flame than earlier oil lamps.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Murdoch demonstrates coal-gas lighting
description: William Murdoch successfully used coal gas for illumination, marking a major step toward urban gas lighting.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: London's Pall Mall is lit by gas
description: Public gas lighting on Pall Mall showed that city streets could be illuminated systematically after dark.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Davy demonstrates the arc lamp
description: Humphry Davy showed that an electric arc between carbon electrodes could produce intense light.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Limelight enters stage lighting
description: Oxyhydrogen-heated lime produced a brilliant spotlight that transformed theatres and public performance.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Kerosene lamps expand domestic lighting
description: The commercial rise of petroleum made kerosene lamps a cheaper and more widespread household light source.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Joseph Swan shows an early practical bulb
description: Swan publicly demonstrated an incandescent lamp using a carbon filament in a partial vacuum.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Edison improves the incandescent lamp
description: Thomas Edison and his team produced a more durable practical incandescent lamp and a system for electric lighting.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Tungsten filament lamps commercialize
description: Tungsten filaments greatly improved efficiency and lifespan compared with carbon-filament bulbs.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Neon lighting is unveiled
description: Georges Claude demonstrated neon lamps, opening the age of electric illuminated signage.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Fluorescent lamps reach the mass market
description: Fluorescent lighting offered higher efficiency than incandescent bulbs and became standard in workplaces and institutions.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: The first practical visible-spectrum LED is made
description: Nick Holonyak Jr. produced the first practical visible LED, opening a new era in solid-state lighting.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Efficient blue LED is achieved
description: Shuji Nakamura's efficient blue LED made bright white LED lighting feasible.

timeline_title: Timeline of Artificial Light
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the technologies humans used to create artificial light, from early oil lamps to modern LED illumination.
title: Blue LED inventors receive the Nobel Prize
description: The Nobel Prize in Physics recognized the blue LED breakthrough that enabled long-lasting, efficient white light sources.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Persian Royal Road courier system operates
description: The Achaemenid Empire maintained relay couriers across the Royal Road, dramatically accelerating state communication over long distances.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: News of Marathon is carried by runner
description: The legendary run associated with Marathon became an enduring symbol of the limits of human-speed message delivery.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Augustus organizes the cursus publicus
description: Rome established an imperial relay post that improved the speed and reliability of official messages across the empire.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Abbasid postal intelligence network expands
description: The barid system linked administration, intelligence, and long-distance communication across the caliphate.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Mongol Yam relay system links Eurasia
description: The Mongol Empire's relay stations enabled unusually rapid communication across a vast territory.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Printing press accelerates information reproduction
description: Movable type did not speed a single courier, but it radically increased how quickly the same message could be multiplied and distributed.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Paris experiments with organized urban post
description: Early city postal reforms showed how scheduled delivery could compress message times inside growing urban centers.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: French semaphore telegraph enters service
description: Claude Chappe's optical telegraph sent messages across relay towers faster than horse couriers over hundreds of miles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Electric telegraph systems are developed
description: Cooke, Wheatstone, and Morse helped turn electricity into a practical medium for near-instant long-distance messaging.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Morse sends the first famous public telegraph message
description: The message 'What hath God wrought' demonstrated that long-distance communication had entered a new era.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Pony Express begins service
description: The Pony Express cut transcontinental U.S. mail delivery to about ten days, setting a high-water mark for horse-based communication.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Transcontinental telegraph ends the Pony Express era
description: Once telegraph lines crossed North America, electronic transmission outpaced even the fastest courier relays.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: A durable transatlantic cable is completed
description: For the first time, messages could routinely cross the Atlantic in minutes instead of weeks.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Telephone is patented
description: Alexander Graham Bell's telephone made real-time voice communication over distance commercially viable.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Wireless radio signaling is demonstrated
description: Marconi's radio work untethered long-distance messaging from wired networks.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: First official transcontinental telephone call is made
description: A coast-to-coast call in the United States showed that real-time voice communication could span a continent.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Telstar relays live transatlantic television
description: Communications satellites brought almost immediate global broadcasting into everyday life.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: ARPANET goes online
description: Packet-switched networking laid the foundations for global digital messaging and the modern internet.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: First handheld cellular phone call is made
description: Mobile telephony started to detach personal communication from fixed locations.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: TAT-8 brings transatlantic fiber-optic service
description: Fiber-optic cable vastly increased the volume and speed of intercontinental data transmission.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: SMS texting begins on public networks
description: Short Message Service made near-instant written communication commonplace for consumers.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: Social media accelerates viral communication
description: Platforms for user-generated networking dramatically reduced the time for information to reach mass audiences.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Velocity of Information
timeline_description: A timeline of major breakthroughs that reduced the time needed to send news and messages across long distances, from couriers to fiber-optic networks.
title: 5G networks begin wider deployment
description: Low-latency mobile broadband further reduced delay in global communication and data exchange.

description: Egyptian evidence shows honey production had become a managed source of sweetness rather than only a wild harvest.

timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness

timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.
title: Aspartame is discovered
description: James Schlatter discovered aspartame, a sweetener that later became widely used in diet foods and beverages.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.
title: High-fructose corn syrup is developed
description: Enzymatic processing made it possible to convert corn syrup into a cheaper industrial sweetener for processed foods.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.
title: Sucralose is discovered

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.
title: Aspartame expands into carbonated drinks
description: Approval for broader beverage use helped turn diet soda into a major consumer category.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.
title: Stevia sweeteners gain wider U.S. acceptance
description: FDA no-objection responses helped plant-derived high-intensity sweeteners enter the mainstream food market.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of Sweetness
timeline_description: A timeline of major shifts in how humans sourced, processed, traded, and engineered sweetness, from honey and sugarcane to industrial sweeteners.
title: Consumer backlash targets added sugar
description: Public-health campaigns and labeling rules pushed manufacturers toward reformulation and lower-sugar products.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Pythagorean discipline links silence to self-mastery
description: Classical traditions treated silence as a moral and intellectual practice rather than merely the absence of sound.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Benedictine monastic rules formalize quiet discipline
description: Monastic life in Christian Europe made regulated silence central to prayer, work, and communal order.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Mechanical clocks make public soundkeeping routine
description: Tower clocks introduced regular artificial sound into urban life through bells and hourly signals.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Printing press amplifies urban information culture
description: Mass print intensified the density of public discourse and reduced the relative rarity of quiet informational spaces.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Addison condemns London noise in The Spectator
description: Early modern observers increasingly treated noise as a civic problem worth public complaint.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Industrial Revolution begins to transform soundscapes
description: Factories, steam power, and dense industrial cities marked a lasting rise in ambient mechanical noise.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Steam railways spread high-volume transport noise
description: Rail transport made machine noise a permanent feature of daily life for towns and cities.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Street organs are targeted in urban anti-noise campaigns
description: Victorian reformers increasingly demanded regulation of intrusive commercial and street sound.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Telephone creates private sound inside the home and office
description: New communication technology moved some speech from public streets into interior, individualized listening spaces.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: The gramophone era expands recorded sound
description: Home audio technology allowed people to bring music and noise deliberately into private domestic spaces.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: New York forms a Noise Abatement Commission
description: One of the first major modern city campaigns treated excessive urban noise as a public-policy issue.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Sound level meters become practical regulatory tools
description: Quantifying noise made modern noise-control policy and standards more enforceable.
source_publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: UN recognizes rest and well-being as social goods
description: Postwar public-health thinking increasingly treated environmental conditions, including noise, as matters of welfare.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Jet travel introduces a new era of aircraft noise
description: The jet age imposed intense noise burdens around airports and along new transport corridors.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: U.S. Noise Control Act is enacted
description: The United States created a national framework for research and standards on environmental noise.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Sony Walkman personalizes listening
description: Portable headphones let individuals retreat from surrounding noise while also normalizing constant personal audio.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Bose develops active noise-canceling aviation headset
description: Electronic noise cancellation offered a technological path back toward engineered quiet.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: WHO issues community-noise guidelines
description: Global health authorities treated environmental noise as a measurable public-health risk.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Consumer noise-canceling headphones reach a wider market
description: Active noise control moved from specialist aviation equipment into everyday consumer electronics.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: WHO publishes environmental noise guidelines for Europe
description: Modern policy increasingly linked chronic noise exposure to cardiovascular and sleep-related harms.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Silence
timeline_description: A timeline of how human environments grew louder and how societies repeatedly tried to protect or recreate silence.
title: Electric vehicles are required to emit warning sounds in many markets
description: The rise of quieter vehicles led regulators to add artificial sound for pedestrian safety.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Babylonian world maps depict a disk-like Earth
description: Some of the earliest surviving world maps represented Earth in schematic flat forms surrounded by water.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Pythagorean thinkers argue for a spherical Earth
description: Greek philosophical speculation began moving influential thought toward a round Earth.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Aristotle offers observational evidence for Earth's curvature
description: He cited eclipses and changing stars with latitude to argue that Earth is spherical.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Eratosthenes estimates Earth's circumference
description: Using shadow measurements, Eratosthenes produced one of antiquity's most famous calculations of Earth's size.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Crates builds a spherical globe
description: The globe of Crates of Mallus showed Earth could be represented as a sphere rather than a flat map.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Ptolemy systematizes geocentric cosmography
description: Ptolemy's synthesis placed a spherical Earth at the center of the universe and shaped learned geography for centuries.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Cosmas Indicopleustes proposes a biblical flat-world model
description: His Christian Topography preserved a notable late antique challenge to the spherical consensus among learned traditions.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Al-Biruni measures Earth with new precision
description: Using trigonometry and horizon observations, al-Biruni refined methods for estimating Earth's radius.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Behaim constructs the oldest surviving terrestrial globe
description: The Erdapfel embodied late medieval European geographic knowledge in three dimensions.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Copernicus places Earth among the planets
description: Heliocentrism changed how Earth's shape and motion were understood within the solar system.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Galileo's telescopic observations support heliocentrism
description: New astronomical evidence weakened the old cosmic status of Earth as the immobile center.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: Newton describes Earth as an oblate spheroid
description: Universal gravitation implied that rotation should slightly flatten Earth at the poles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in how humans described, measured, and visualized the shape of Earth, from ancient cosmologies to satellite geodesy.
title: French geodesic expeditions test Earth's flattening
description: Expeditions to Lapland and Peru gathered measurements that supported Newton's oblate model.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Shape of the Earth
